_comment: Epic N (ADR 0035). N-S1 + N-S2 LANDED. N-S1: C++ methods are qualified by their enclosing class (`Calculator::run` distinct from `Widget::run`); the intra-file resolver is scope-aware (a bare `tick()` inside `Widget::run` resolves to `Widget::tick`, not Calculator's). N-S2: a call the intra-file pass can't resolve binds to the UNIQUE non-static free-function definition in another file (`total -> util.cpp#shared_util` is now a resolved `direct/likely` cross-file edge — header-declares/.cpp-defines/called-elsewhere via C++ global linkage), so `main` reaches the cross-file functions. Ceiling stays `likely` (tree-sitter, no type system); ambiguous (overloaded) or undefined names stay `unresolved`. The private non-static `value()` is omitted from `exported` — the coarse `!static` heuristic over-reports it; a later stage may refine access-specifier grading.
